core_mechanism: geometric phase (holonomy / anholonomy): the state of a system parallel-transported adiabatically around a closed loop in its parameter space acquires a phase determined only by the geometry of the loop (the enclosed solid angle/area), independent of the traversal rate and separable from the dynamical phase
brief_reason: The abstract reports the controlled accumulation of Berry's geometric phase by manipulating the system geometrically around a closed path and reading it out via interference, distinguishing it from dynamic effects — the defining signature of holonomy under adiabatic cyclic transport.
